codeReview.summary: The change strengthens service-level parity assertions, but it does not satisfy the requested ViewInspector-driven Dashboard→SessionDetail→ContextPackSheet harness hardening. The new test still compares `ContextPackService.assemble` outputs directly and never exercises the real entry surfaces, and the added parity checks stop short of full ordered session-id equality across entrypoints.

issues.description: This new test still uses `assembleAnchored`/`assembleUnanchored` helpers, which call `ContextPackService.assemble` directly, so it never instantiates `DashboardView`, `SessionDetailView`, or `ContextPackSheet`. That means the feature's required real ViewInspector-driven harness for CTXDETAIL/CTXCROSS is still missing.

The "explicit ordered parity" coverage only checks set equality, per-path stability, first-position parity, and same-project adjacency; it never asserts full ordered `sessionIds` equality between the two entrypoints. Mid-array ordering drift would still pass these tests, so the requested ordered parity coverage remains incomplete.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `context-pack-ui-worker` procedure already says cross-flow assertions must drive Dashboard → SessionDetail → ContextPackSheet (or an equivalent real view harness) and that service-only tests do not satisfy CTXDETAIL/CTXCROSS coverage, but this feature still implemented only `ContextPackService`-level parity checks.

summary: Review failed: the commit strengthens service-level parity assertions, but it does not add the requested real UI harness coverage or full ordered cross-entry parity. No guidance/documentation change was made in the repo; the existing skill guidance already requires the real view-surface harness.
